note: Shared list locking the risky-pattern heuristic across runtimes. `risky` are patterns QRID_riskyPattern / CheckCharacter::riskyPattern must reject (config error, no compile); `safe` must pass through. The gate has two stages. Stage one rejects nested quantifiers (bounded and unbounded) AND any repetition of a group that can match ambiguously — alternation, optionals, or inner quantifiers — with inner groups collapsed layer by layer so nesting cannot hide the shape. Stage two-a rejects the POLYNOMIAL class stage one misses: two or more UNBOUNDED quantifiers (*, +, {n,}) over overlapping character classes with no mandatory separator between them (.*.*, [0-9]*[0-9]*, [A-Z]+[A-Z0-9]+). Stage two-b (F1) rejects a contiguous run of overlapping BOUNDED quantifiers whose product of per-factor match-length choices exceeds MAX_BACKTRACK_PRODUCT (1e6) — e.g. A{1,20}A{1,20}A{1,20}A{1,20}A{1,20} (=3.2M): each factor is bounded but the factor count is the backtracking exponent, so a long chain freezes a browser's engine even though PCRE2 auto-possessifies it, and unlike the server the client has no match-time backstop. It deliberately over-rejects (a repeated alternation is refused even with disjoint branches; a collapsed group is treated as overlapping everything). Still a heuristic, not a proof: a SHORT bounded backtracker under the budget like A{1,40}A{1,40}A{1,40}9 (=64000) passes, which is why the server keeps the match-time PCRE-error guard as a backstop. `risky_js.cjs` additionally times every `safe` pattern against adversarial input, so "safe" is measured, not assumed.
